texto: When I auditioned for drama college, they asked me to do my Shakespeare. I couldn't do it. They asked me to do my modern, and I couldn't do it. They asked me if I had a song prepared, and I said 'No,' so I sang 'Happy Birthday.' And I did a reasonable improvisation, a reasonable one, nothing special at all. I don't know how I got in, but I did.
explicacao: Citação de Hans Matheson sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “When I auditioned for drama college, they asked me to do my Shakespeare. I couldn't do it. They a…” articula felicidade, happy, college em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como aniversario e college.

frase_original: When I auditioned for drama college, they asked me to do my Shakespeare. I couldn't do it. They asked me to do my modern, and I couldn't do it. They asked me if I had a song prepared, and I said 'No,' so I sang 'Happy Birthday.' And I did a reasonable improvisation, a reasonable one, nothing special at all. I don't know how I got in, but I did.

texto: Playing characters who are wonderful and beautiful is hard because you don't feel like that most of the time... well, I don't. It's like this whole heart-throb nonsense. It's flattering, but that's not how I feel in the morning. It's something that goes with the job.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hans Matheson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Playing characters who are wonderful and beautiful is hard because you don't feel like that most …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, time, beautiful pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e beautiful.

frase_original: Playing characters who are wonderful and beautiful is hard because you don't feel like that most of the time... well, I don't. It's like this whole heart-throb nonsense. It's flattering, but that's not how I feel in the morning. It's something that goes with the job.
